texts.full_text: during the development of a basal chordate. Tunicates are unique animals for studying the origin and evolution of vertebrates because they are considered vertebrates' closest living relatives and share the vertebrate body plan and many specific features. The coronal organ represents the best candidate homolog for the vertebrate lateral line. To further understand the evolution of secondary sensory cells, we analysed the development and cytodifferentiation of coronal cells in the tunicate ascidian Ciona intestinalis for the first time. Here, coronal sensory cells can be identified as early as larval metamorphosis, before tentacles form, as cells with short cilia and The text discusses neural placodes, transient structures that develop into sensory cells in various organisms. It highlights similarities between vertebrates' hair cells and tunicates' coronal organ in terms of their apical apparatuses with cilia and stereovilli or microvilli. texts.full_text: pairing of 16 year old Cameron Humphreys and Belgian international Jason Denayer started the game, flanked on either side by Sagna and Kolarov. Joe Hart lined up behind them between the sticks. Unfortunately it turned into a relatively sobering experience. Real Madrid opted for pretty much their strongest XI, and it told. For all their talents, the Barclays u21 league and the Scottish Premier Division won't quite prepare you for the experience of facing Benzema, Bale and Ronaldo for the first time. Not many things can really, but that's football and it's all part of their development. Denayer and Humphreys were hardly awful, but their hesitancy and naivety was evident as they picked up a full 90 minutes each. Both were comfortable in possession, as ever, though both struggled with picking up the precise and varied movements of Madrid's frontline. Denayer, in particular, looked out of his depth at times, but it's important to remember that he's still only 19 years old and has plenty of time to develop. texts.full_text: an active duty Judge Advocate (JAG) for the United States Air Force (USAF) for over six years, separating in September 2017. Since joining Patriots Law Group, he has practiced in Maryland, Virginia, the District of Columbia (DC), and New York on civil and/or criminal matters, as well as in Federal court, and in the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) and Merit Systems Protection Board (MSPB) on employment matters. He is admitted to practice in Maryland, Virginia, DC, New York, the Air Force Court of Criminal Appeals, the Court of Appeals for the Armed Forces, the Court of Federal Claims, the District Court for DC, and the Supreme Court of the United States. He is also accredited to practice on Veterans Affairs Appeals. During his time in service, after graduating from Commissioned Officer Training as a distinguished graduate, he followed a litigation track, beginning with prosecuting those accused of criminal violations under the Uniform Code of Military Justice (UCMJ) at Yokota Air Base, Japan. texts.full_text: space^[@CR8],\ [@CR14],\ [@CR15]^, and due to the presence of interface states. The latter is called the Rashba effect (see Figure~1a). Therefore a TI surface has two types of SOT: field-like torque and damping-like torque. ![](https://i.imgur.com/0e8o4Jc.png) Figure~\ref{Fig1} | Spin-orbit torques in topological insulators. (a) A schematic diagram showing the Rashba effect, where a spin accumulation is induced due to the Berry curvature of the surface states and out-of-plane SOC at the interface. The direction of the charge current ($j$), momentum ($k$), spin texture ($S$), and spin density ($\sigma$) are indicated in red, blue, green, and purple respectively. (b) A schematic diagram showing the spin accumulation induced by the spin-momentum locking effect. Here $E_{F}$ is Fermi level. The SOT strength depends on the SOC strength of TI materials. In topological insulators with strong SOC such as Bi$_{2-x}$Sb$_x$Te$_3$, a large field-like torque and damping-like torque can be obtained^[@CR8]^. However, in weak SOC TIs like HgCdTe alloys (HCT), the SOT is too small to be detected. The spin accumulation induced by the Rashba effect is proportional to the charge current flowing on TI surface as a result of the Berry curvature and out-of-plane SOC at the interface^[@CR8]^. Therefore, the strength of this type of torque depends only on the Fermi level ($E_{F}$)~\cite{PhysRevLett.117.246601}. The spin accumulation induced by the spin-momentum locking effect is proportional to $j$ and $\hbar k$, where $k$ is wavevector, as a result of the surface states' spin texture (see Figure~1b). This type of torque depends on both Fermi level ($E_{F}$) and charge current density ($j$), therefore it can be tuned by varying these parameters. The strength of this SOT effect also depends on the SOC at TI interface, which is proportional to $k$. Thus one needs a strong SOC system in order to obtain a large spin accumulation. In summary, there are two types of SOT in TIs: field-like torque a

texts.full_text: and beta-PLD forms. Expotentially growing and plateau-phase V79 cells were exposed to various doses of neutrons and plated either immediately or after treatment in medium (250-500 mM NaCl) to express radiation-induced potentially lethal damage (PLD). Postirradiation treatment of exponentially growing cells in medium (500 mM) resulted in a decrease in both Dq and D0, postirradiation treatment of plateau-phase cells in hypertonic medium led to an increase in Dq and no change in D0. These results suggest that the expression of PLD is influenced by cell cycle phase and hypertonic treatment. To investigate this, we examined the distinction between alpha (α) and beta (β)-PLD forms in plateau-phase cells after exposure to neutrons or gamma rays followed by hypertonic treatment. Our findings indicate that: 1. Neutron Irradiation: Postirradiation treatment with 500 mM NaCl increased the proportion of β-PLD, while α-PLD remained relatively constant. This suggests that neutrons induce more in plateau-phase cells, which is then expressed upon hypertonic treatment. 2. texts.full_text: stoichiometry (i.e., ideal air-to-fuel ratio). This requires that the in-cylinder air charge (i.e., mass flow rate of air into the cylinder) be matched by an appropriate amount of fuel. At each engine event, in-cylinder air-charge is typically estimated based on the measurements from a throttle mass air flow (MAF) sensor or an intake manifold pressure (MAP) sensor. However, the present air-charge estimate, which pertains to the cylinder presently on the intake stroke, is several (typically one or two) engine events late for a fueling decision. This happens because the optimal timing for fuel injection in port fuel injection engines is on the closed intake valve. Moreover, dispensing the fuel takes a finite amount of time and larger quantities at higher engine speed may not be dispensed in one event or less. Thus, the amount of fuel decided at time t will be dispensed into the port of a cylinder that is to start its intake several engine events into the future. An improvement in the ability to control air/fuel ratio will follow if future values of cylinder air-charge can be predicted based on the present and past measurement of engine operating conditions. LID is a significant motor complication, and understanding the dysregulation of the Ras-guanine nucleotide-releasing factor 1 (Ras-GRF1) and the extracellular signal-regulated kinase (ERK) dependent striatal plasticity is a crucial step towards uncovering the pathophysiology of the dyskinesia and the underlying pathology of the disease. Ras-GRF1 is a crucial upstream activator of the Ras-ERK signaling pathway. This pathway is a key mediator of the plasticity and the dysregulation of the Ras-GRF1 is implicated in the pathology of L-DOPA-induced dyskinesia. This dysregulation is of particular interest in the context of L-DOPA-induced dyskinesia. L-DOPA is the standard of care in the treatment of Parkinson's disease. texts.full_text: from teen-age drivers Thank you for The Sun's coverage of the Johns Hopkins School of Public Health's study that confirms previous reports that the chance of a fatal crash rises significantly when 16- and 17-year-old drivers are transporting three or more teen-age passengers ("Passengers increase peril in teen driving," March 22). The article referred to legislation that I introduced this year to limit the number of teen-age passengers to no more than two, when 16- or 17-year-old provisionally licensed drivers are behind the wheel and to require their passengers to buckle up. For the record, I did not withdraw my bill. I combined it with similar legislation put forth by the General Assembly's task force on driver's education programs. While the teen-age passenger limit language was removed in committee, we preserved what I believe is the bill's most valuable section, which requires all passengers of provisionally licensed drivers to buckle up. This will, in effect, limit the passengers to the number of safety belts available. If passed, this legislation will surely increase seat belt use among our most at-risk passengers and prevent many of the tragic deaths and injuries so frequently associated with teen driving. William A. Bronrott, Annapolis The writer represents Montgomery County in the Maryland House of Delegates. Coverage of escape put hostages in jeopardy Thankful that

We will focus on the C57B6J mouse as the background strain for these studies because: 1) it is has a well-described tendency toward dietary obesity and, 2) it is the background strain for ob/ob and db/db mice. We will evaluate: 1) the ability of leptin to modify the feedback potency of oral and GI food stimuli in ob/ob mice, 2) the ability of transgenic neuron-specific replacement of LEPR-B to restore normal processing of oral and GI food stimuli in db/db mice, and 3) the effect of central vs. peripheral inducible LEPR-B deficiency on eating in mice. texts.full_text: n() { std::cout<<sizeof(A); } InIn C++, a compiler does not automatically make a virtual function non-virtual even when it might seem possible to do so, primarily due to the principles of polymorphism and the need to maintain the object-oriented design and behavior expected by the programmer. When a class has a virtual function, the compiler typically adds a virtual table (vtable) to the class. This vtable is a table of function pointers that point to the virtual functions of the class. The vtable allows the program to support polymorphism, enabling the correct function to be called at runtime based on the actual object type, not the type of the pointer or reference used to call the function. Here's a breakdown of why a virtual function remains virtual even when it might seem unnecessary: 1. Polymorphism and Inheritance: Virtual functions are used to implement polymorphism, which is a key feature of object-oriented programming. If a function is virtual in a base class, it is expected to be overridden in derived classes. The development of polymeric EO modulators represents a substantial improvement over traditional devices that typically require 5 volts for halfwave operation. The key innovation lies in modifying the structure of organic chromophores used within these polymers. These modifications, referred to as steric modification, reduce intermolecular interactions which can otherwise lead to attenuation of EO activity induced by electric field poling. By incorporating such modified chromophores into polymer hosts, researchers have managed to fabricate modulators that achieve halfwave voltages as low as 0.8 volts at a telecommunications wavelength of 1318 nanometers. texts.full_text: Corynebacterium glutamicum. Corynebacterium glutamicum has been engineered for production of the polyamide monomer putrescine or 1,4-diaminobutane. Here, N-acetylputrescine was shown to be a significant by-product of putrescine production by recombinant putrescine producing C. glutamicum strains. A systematic gene deletion approach of 18 (putative) N-acetyltransferase genes revealed that the cg1722 gene product was responsible for putrescine acetylation. The encoded enzyme was purified and characterized as polyamine N-acetyltransferase. The enzyme accepted acetyl-CoA and propionyl-CoA as donors for acetylation of putrescine and other diamines as acceptors, but showed highest catalytic efficiency with the triamine spermidine and the tetraamine spermine and, hence, was named SnaA. Upon deletion of snaA in the putrescine producing strain PUT21, no acteylputrescine accumulated, but about 41% more putrescine as compared to the parent strain. Moreover, a transcriptome approach identified increased expression of the cgmAR operon encoding a putative permease and a transcriptional TetR-family repressor upon induction of putrescine production in C. glutamicum PUT21. texts.full_text: timestamp specific data from a flatfile I have a huge file containing a list like this email@domain.com^B1569521698 email2@domain.com,@2domain.com^B1569521798 email3@domain.com,test@2domain.com^B1569521898 email10000@domain.com^B1569521998 .. .. The file is named /usr/local/email/whitelist The number after ^B is a unix timestamp I need to remove from the list all the rows having a timestamp smaller than (e.g.) 1569521898. I tried using various awk/sed combinations with no result. A: The character ^B you notice is a control character. The first 32 control-characters which are ASCII codes 0 through 1FH, form a special set of non-printing characters. These characters are called the control characters because these characters perform various printer and display control operations rather than displaying symbols. This particular one stands for STXand it is used to mark the beginning of a new line in some file formats, including the one you're dealing with. Here's how you can use `awk` to filter out lines based on their unix timestamp: ``` awk -F'[^[:space:]]+' '{split($2,arr,"^B"); if(arr[1]>=1569521898) print}' /usr/local/email/whitelist > temp && mv temp /usr/local/email/whitelist ``` This command does the following: - `-F'[^[:space:]]+'`: sets the field separator to anything that is not whitespace. This means it will split on the `^B` character. - `{split($2,arr,"^B"); if(arr[1]>=1569521898) print}'`: for each line, it splits the second field (indexed as `$2`) on the `^B` character and checks if the first element of the resulting array is greater than or equal to 1569521898. If it is, it prints the entire line. - `>/temp && mv temp /usr/local/email/whitelist`: redirects the output to a temporary file (`temp`), then moves that temporary file into place of the original

Some operations, however, are still not automated. For example, detection of counterfeit and damaged chips or chips belonging to another casino is done visually. Although there have been efforts to provide chips with means so that different types of chips can visually be easily and quickly identified, the chips are still visually inspected. See, for example, U.S. Pat. No. 3,350,802 (Segel) issued Nov. 7, 1967, U.S. Pat. No. 3,953,932 (Graves) issued May 4, 1976, U.S. Pat. No. 3,968,582 (Jones) issued Jul. 13, 1976, U.S. Pat. No. 4,435,911 (Jones) issued Mar. 13, 1984, and U.S. Design Patent Nos. 232,367, 237,724 and 240,053 (all of Garaventa) issued Aug. 13, 1974, Nov. 18, 1975 and May 25, 1976, respectively. It is known to provide chips with means so that the presence of the chips can automatically be detected. See for example, the 582 Patent (Jones) and U.S. Pat.
